# Fire Bolt

- **Level**: Cantrip (0)
- **School**: Evocation
- **Casting Time**: 1 action
- **Range**: 120 feet
- **Components**: V, S
- **Duration**: Instantaneous
- **Classes**: Sorcerer, Wizard, Artificer

## Description

You hurl a mote of fire at a creature or object within range.  Make a
ranged spell attack against the target.  On a hit, the target takes
1d10 fire damage.  A flammable object hit by this spell ignites if it
is not being worn or carried.

## At Higher Levels

This spell's damage increases by 1d10 when you reach 5th level
(2d10), 11th level (3d10), and 17th level (4d10).

## Tactical Notes

- **Single-target cantrip**: Unlike Fireball's AOE, Fire Bolt hits
  only one creature.  It is the standard "I do not want to spend a
  slot" damage option for low-level sorcerers and wizards.
- **No save, no attack mod**: Fire Bolt uses a ranged spell attack,
  so it punishes low-AC targets but whiffs against high-AC frontliners.
- **Object ignition**: Useful for puzzles involving ropes, wooden
  barriers, or oil-soaked floors.  An ignited object takes 1d4 fire
  damage at the start of each of its turns.

## Cross-References

- See `fireball.md` for the spell-slot AOE version.  Fire Bolt is
  the cantrip companion — same damage type, no resource cost.
